steps.title: Identify the key claim
steps.content: The text argues that history knowledge "must not be left to historians" — meaning it is essential and must be shared with the public, not confined to specialists. The blank must describe what the author is rejecting: the idea that such knowledge is something it should not be.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
The correct word must mean something like "unnecessary" or "needless" — the view the author opposes:
  • Choice A — 'unattainable': Means impossible to achieve; the argument is about who should have access, not whether it is achievable. ✗
  • Choice B — 'commonplace': Means ordinary or widespread; this does not capture the idea of being unnecessary or restricted. ✗
  • Choice C — 'equitable': Means fair or just; saying history knowledge is "not equitable" would be a different and unrelated claim. ✗
  • Choice D — 'superfluous': Means unnecessary or excessive; if history knowledge is "not superfluous," it is necessary — exactly the argument that it must be shared beyond specialists. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
